remarks.text: Finance analytics and AI: how decision intelligence platforms improve FP&A, risk, and reporting
remarks.content: Finance teams have more data than they can act on. Transactions stream in from enterprise resource planning (ERP), general ledger (GL), accounts payable and receivable (AP/AR), market feeds, and treasury systems around the clock. But by the time that data reaches a decision, the moment h
remarks.contentTranslation: 财务团队拥有的数据太多，无法采取行动。交易全天候从企业资源规划（ ERP ）、总分类账（ GL ）、应付账款和应收账款（ AP/AR ）、市场馈送和财务系统流入。但当数据做出决定时，时刻h
remarks.translation: 财务分析和AI：决策智能平台如何改善财务规划与资产管理、风险和报告

remarks.text: From partnership to pipeline: how Snowflake and Dataiku drove 200%+ YoY sourced opportunity growth
remarks.content: Partnerships are often judged too early, or on the wrong things. Visibility. Executive alignment. Event presence. A well-worded press release. None of that is the real test.
remarks.contentTranslation: 人们往往过早地判断伙伴关系，或者判断错误。可见性。高管一致性。事件存在。措辞良好的新闻稿。这些都不是真正的考验。
remarks.translation: 从合作伙伴关系到渠道： Snowflake和Dataiku如何推动200% +同比机会增长

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Alteryx data analytics in 2026: strengths, limitations, and alternatives
remarks.content: In our view, Alteryx built its reputation on a simple promise: Let business analysts do data work that used to require SQL, Python, or a request to IT. For years, that promise held. Drag-and-drop workflows replaced manual spreadsheet processes. Teams that waited weeks for data engineerin
remarks.contentTranslation: 我们认为， Alteryx的声誉建立在一个简单的承诺之上：让业务分析师做过去需要SQL、Python或IT请求的数据工作。多年来，这个承诺一直保持着。拖放工作流取代了手动电子表格流程。等待数据工程师数周的团队
remarks.translation: 2026年Alteryx数据分析：优势、局限性和替代方案

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
The barrier for non-technical people using coding agents was never the interface. Chatting is the easiest UI ever invented

The barrier is that they don't know what to ask for.

The blank chat box assumes you already know what's possible. Most people don't.

When I tried Town, I was impressed by its onboarding process where the agent proactively suggested workflows & things it can take off my plate, rather than waiting for me to give instructions
remarks.translation: 非技术人员使用编码Agent的障碍从来不是界面。聊天是有史以来发明的最简单的用户界面。障碍在于他们不知道要什么。空白的聊天框假设您已经知道什么是可能的。大多数人不知道。当我尝试Town时，我对它的入职流程印象深刻，客服代表主动提出工作流和可以从我的盘子里拿走的东西，而不是等待我给出指示
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
This is a critical post to read if you’re building an applied AI company right now.

“An application earns its place in the untrainable corner by doing unglamorous work: arranging a company's private reality so a model can act on it, handing the model the tools to act, working with the customer to change the reality of its workforce. A company that brings the translation is tough to copy – and the translation never ends. Integration and maintenance run as long as the relationship does, won by teams that put domain-specialized engineers and tools next to the customer.”

There’s still an insanely large gulf between model capabilities and what it takes to apply them to specific corporate workflows. Some of that is technology that needs to be built, a lot is access to (and formatting of) the right data to work with, and a ton more is on the change management and specific implementation work (FDEs, etc.) it takes to make AI work in any specific corporate setting. 

2 things can be very true at once: frontier models and labs will continue to grow an incredible amount, and there will be a vast ecosystem of software and services companies that emerge to bring the power of these models to real enterprises. This makes room for new infrastructure provides, applied AI companies in every vertical, new versions of system integrators, and more players.

Incredibly exciting time on all fronts.
remarks.translation:
如果您现在正在建立一家应用AI公司，这是一篇值得阅读的重要帖子。“应用程序通过做平淡无奇的工作在无法训练的角落赢得自己的位置：安排公司的私人现实，以便模型可以根据它采取行动，将模型作为采取行动的工具，与客户合作改变其员工的现实。带来翻译的公司很难复制–翻译永远不会结束。
只要这种关系存在，集成和维护就可以运行，这得益于将专业领域的工程师和工具放在客户旁边的团队。“模型功能与将其应用于特定企业工作流之间存在着巨大的差距。
其中一些是需要构建的技术，很多是访问（和格式化）正确的数据，还有很多是关于变革管理和具体实施工作（ FDE等） ，使AI在任何特定的企业环境中工作。
有两件事可能同时非常真实：前沿模型和实验室将继续增长令人难以置信的数量，并且将出现一个庞大的软件和服务公司生态系统，将这些模型的力量带给真正的企业。这为新的基础设施、每个垂直领域的应用AI公司、新版本的系统集成商以及更多的参与者提供了空间。各条战线上令人难以置信的激动人心的时刻。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews to help busy people become AI builders
remarks.text:
Btw this is the prompt I used:

Build F-Zero: futuristic anti-gravity racer with pseudo-3D track (raycasting or mode-7 style scaling), 3 AI opponents, boost meter that drains health, speed 400-800 km/h with visible sense of speed via track warping and scrolling ground pattern, arrow keys to steer, shift to boost, 3 laps on a looping track with checkpoints. 

Style it neon cyberpunk (dark sky, glowing track edges, chromatic aberration on boost), show HUD with position/lap/speed/health, and make the ship bank into turns. Prioritize feel over realism - the speed sensation is the whole game.
remarks.translation:
顺便说一句，这是我使用的提示：构建F-Zero ：具有伪3D轨道的未来主义反重力赛车（射线投射或模式-7风格缩放） ， 3个AI对手，消耗健康的提升仪，通过轨道翘曲和滚动地面模式具有可见速度感的400-800公里/小时的速度，转向的箭头键，转向提升，在有检查点的循环轨道上3圈。
设计霓虹灯赛博朋克（黑暗的天空，发光的轨道边缘，提升时的色差） ，显示位置/圈/速度/健康的HUD ，并使船舶转弯。优先考虑感觉而不是现实主义-速度感是整个游戏。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

I was listening to the latest episode of @InvestLikeBest and it talked a lot about S-curves..

Live now on escurves dot com 🌊 https://t.co/jYMCkJde9x
remarks.translation: 我正在听最新一集的@ InvestLikeBest ，它谈论了很多关于S曲线的事情。所以我让Fable ONE拍摄了一个网站，讨论了所有的S曲线，它们的拐点和对每个曲线的评论都是过去200年的泡沫。现已在escurves dot com上线 🌊

remarks.name: Simon Willison's Blog
remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: Initial impressions of Claude Fable 5
remarks.content: I didn't have early access to today's Claude Fable 5 release, but I've spent the past ~5.5 hours putting it through its paces. My initial impressions are that this is something of a beast . It's slow, expensive and has b

remarks.text:
I will be doing a virtual talk this Friday on my vibe coding process. 

As a non-technical person who has somehow managed to get 30k stars on GitHub, I will be sharing the behind-the-scenes of how I get product ideas, how I work with coding agents, how to design stuff that's not AI slop, and why I think code is a medium for storytelling. 

RSVP here https://t.co/4wxpJjrAAB (Use the code PREMIUMPASS under "Add a coupon" to access the event for free)

(Thanks to @sariazout and the @wwwsublimeapp  community for hosting)
remarks.translation:
我将在本周五就我的vibe编码过程进行一次虚拟演讲。作为一个在GitHub上设法获得3万颗星的非技术人员，我将分享我如何获得产品创意的幕后花絮，我如何与编码Agent合作，如何设计非AI SLOP的东西，以及为什么我认为代码是讲故事的媒介。
请在此处回复（使用「添加礼金券」下的优惠码PREMIUMPASS免费参加活动） （感谢@ sariazout和@ wwwsublimeapp社区的出租）

remarks.name: Alex Albert
remarks.role: Research AnthropicAI. Opinions are my own!
remarks.text:
We've reset usage limits across our products! 

For those just starting to test Fable, here's four tips for using it more effectively:
1. Give it bigger, more ambitious tasks than what previous models could handle.
2. Use xhigh/high effort as your default for best performance, med for faster interactive sessions.
3. Rework your skills and CLAUDE.mds. Instructions written for prior models anchor Fable to stale patterns, let it use its own judgment first.
4. Move from providing tasks to providing objectives. Describe what done looks like and how to verify it, then let Fable find the path (/loop and /goal are built for this)
remarks.translation:
我们已重置所有产品的使用限制！对于刚刚开始测试寓言的人来说，以下是更有效地使用寓言的四个提示： 1.给它更大、更雄心勃勃的任务，而不是以前的模型可以处理的任务。2.使用xhigh/high effort作为默认值以获得最佳性能， MED用于更快的互动会话。3.重塑您的技能和CLAUDE.mds。为先前模型编写的说明将寓言锚定到陈旧的模式，让它首先使用自己的判断。4.
从提供任务转向提供目标。描述完成的样子以及如何验证，然后让Fable找到路径（/loop和/goal就是为此构建的）

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text:
Fable 5 is the biggest step up I’ve felt in our models since Opus 4.5 back in November. After 4.5 came out I uninstalled my IDE when I realized that I’d been doing 100% of my coding in a terminal for a few weeks. With Fable, it’s felt like Claude has stepped up from being a coding agent to a thought and design partner in building the product. Fable has judgement, taste, and dimensionality in a way that previous models didn’t, leading me to trust it more with the most complex work.

I think the first time I had this realization was when I asked Fable to debug something. It is the first model I have used that was so methodical and precise, taking measurements and adding logs then verifying that it truly fixed the issue before declaring victory.

There’s nothing in claude code’s prompting telling the model to do that, it’s just part of its personality. It really has this “big model smell” that I haven’t felt before.
remarks.translation:
自去年11月Opus 4.5以来，寓言5是我在我们的车型中感受到的最大的进步。4.5出来后，我卸载了我的IDE ，当我意识到我已经在终端上完成了几周的100 ％的编码。在Fable中， Claude感觉自己已经从一个编码Agent变成了构建产品的思想和设计合作伙伴。
寓言具有判断力、品味和维度，这是以前的模型所没有的，这使我更加信任它完成最复杂的工作。我想我第一次意识到这一点时，我让寓言调试了一些东西。这是我使用的第一个如此有条不紊和精确的模型，在宣布胜利之前进行测量并添加日志，然后验证它是否真正解决了问题。
Claude Code并没有提示模特这样做，这只是模特个性的一部分。它真的有一种我以前从未感受到的“大模特气味”。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text:
We talk a lot about how important it is to set up self-verification loops. Especially in the age of powerful models that can run for long periods of time, self-verification is a key ingredient that enables the model to run for much longer, delivering a result that is closer to what you intended, so you can do more without having to constantly check in on Claude as it works.

@delba_oliveira gives a great breakdown of what that looks like and why it matters
remarks.translation: 我们经常谈论设置自我验证循环的重要性。特别是在可以长时间运行的强大模型时代，自我验证是使模型运行更长时间的关键因素，其结果更接近您的预期，因此您可以做得更多，而无需不断检查Claude的工作。@ delba_oliveira详细介绍了它的外观及其重要性
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Andrej Karpathy
remarks.role: I like training large deep neural nets.
remarks.text:
This is a super exciting release - Claude Fable 5 is the same underlying model as Mythos but with added safeguards. The benchmarks are great and it's SOTA on everything by a margin but I'll add that *qualitatively* also, this is a major-version-bump-deserving step change forward (imo of the same order as Claude 4.5 was in November), peaking especially for long problem-solving sessions on very difficult problems. You can give it a lot more ambitious tasks than what you're used to, the model "gets it" and it will just go, and it's never felt this tempting to stop looking at the code at all (but don't do this in prod!). The model still has quirks that people will run into and the safeguards are configured to be a little too trigger happy for launch, which can hopefully be tuned over time.

I feel a lot of things changing as working software increasingly comes out on a tap. The Jevon's paradox kicks in and I feel my own demand for software growing substantially. You can ask for anything - explainers, visualizers, dashboards, bespoke single-use apps (e.g. a full wandb that is hyper-specific just for your project), you can 10X your test suite, auto-optimize code, run giant research projects with custom HTML for the results, anything! "Free your mind" (Matrix ref). Really looking forward to all the things people build!
remarks.translation:
这是一个超级令人兴奋的版本- Claude Fable 5与Mythos相同的基础模型，但具有额外的保护措施。基准很棒，在所有方面都是SOTA ，但我要补充的是， *从质量上讲* ，这是一个值得向前迈出的重要一步（与Claude 4.5在11月份的顺序相同的国际海事组织） ，特别是对于非常困难的问题的长期解决问题会议。
你可以给它一个比你习惯的更雄心勃勃的任务，模型“得到它” ，它就会消失，而且从来没有感觉到停止查看代码的诱惑（但不要在生产中这样做！ ）。该模型仍然存在人们会遇到的怪癖，并且防护措施被配置为对发布有点过于高兴，希望可以随着时间的推移进行调整。随着工作软件越来越多地出现在水龙头上，我觉得很多事情都在发生变化。
Jevon的悖论开始了，我觉得我自己对软件的需求大幅增长。您可以要求任何东西-解释器，可视化工具，仪表板，定制的一次性应用程序（例如，仅针对您的项目的超特定的完整wandb ） ，您可以10倍的测试套件，自动优化代码，使用自定义HTML运行巨大的研究项目以获得结果，任何东西！ “解放你的思想” （矩阵参考）。非常期待人们建造的所有东西！

remarks.name: Alex Albert
remarks.role: Research AnthropicAI. Opinions are my own!
remarks.text:
I've been at Anthropic through every model launch. There's been a few cases I can remember of a launch that stands out and marks a step-change in how we use models:
- Claude Opus 3
- Claude Sonnet 3.5
- Claude Opus 4.5

And now Claude Fable 5.

With Fable, the model stopped feeling like a tool I direct and started feeling more like something I collaborate with.
remarks.translation: 我在Anthropic的每次模特发布中都参加过。我记得有一些案例让我印象深刻，标志着我们使用模型的方式发生了变化： - Claude Opus 3 - Claude Sonnet 3.5 - Claude Opus 4.5 ，现在是Claude Fable 5。有了寓言，这个模型不再感觉像是我指导的工具，而是开始感觉更像是我合作的东西。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
Great post. So much about model performance is a function of how much compute you’re doing at inference time. This means compute-normalized benchmarks is the only logical path forward. 

And yet, the challenge is it’s a lot harder than it seems given it’s subjective how much compute to apply, which means models behave differently at different thresholds (simplistically, model X’s min thinking may beat model Y’s min thinking, but be reversed at high), and there are a near infinite set of thresholds you could choose to set. 

But either way, moving more in this direction would be great for better understanding AI progress.
remarks.translation:
很棒的帖子。模型性能很大程度上取决于您在推理时进行的计算量。这意味着计算归一化基准是前进的唯一逻辑路径。
然而，挑战在于它比看起来要困难得多，因为它主观地考虑了应用多少计算，这意味着模型在不同的阈值上表现不同（简单地说，模型X的最小思维可能胜过模型Y的最小思维，但在高处被颠倒） ，并且您可以选择设置一组近乎无限的阈值。但无论哪种方式，朝着这个方向前进都将有助于更好地理解AI的进步。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: Ep 89: AI Research Legend’s Honest Assessment of Where We Are
podcasts.excerpt: This episode with Lukasz Kaiser, co-author of the seminal "Attention Is All You Need" transformer paper and former researcher at both Google Brain and OpenAI, is a wide-ranging conversation about the fundamental limits of current AI architectures and whether transformers will continue to dominate or eventually give way to something new. Lukasz brings a rare dual perspective: deep belief in how far the current paradigm has taken us (he's an enthusiastic daily Codex user who's seen 10x productivity gains in his own research), while maintaining genuine intellectual humility about whether transformers can truly generalize the way humans do. The episode weaves together questions about data efficiency, the non-verifiable RL frontier, the coding agent revolution, the open vs. closed source gap, and what the next architectural leap might look like: all filtered through the lens of someone who helped bu…

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: 🔬ESM: The Bitter Lesson is Coming for Proteins - Alex Rives, BioHub
podcasts.excerpt: Editor’s note: In our first BioHub pod with Priscilla and Mark they discussed their acquisition of EvoScale, led by Alex Rives, who is now Head of Science at BioHub. With ESM-1 they trained language models on millions of protein sequences drawn from across life, with a simple “next token” objective: predict the amino acids that have been randomly masked out, based on the context of the rest of the sequence. But they soon found that these models also learned biological structure and function, including properties the model had never been explicitly s…

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: We Automated Everything With AI and Tripled Our Headcount
podcasts.excerpt: Dan Shipper runs one of the most AI-native companies today. Every has agents embedded in nearly every workflow—“if you swing a stick in our Slack, you're as likely to hit a human as an agent,” he says. And yet the company has grown from four people to 30 since GPT-3 came out, and is still hiring.Why does Dan believe there's more human work to do than ever?In a format flip for AI & I, Every's COO Brandon Gell turns the tables and interviews Dan about his latest essay, “After Automation”—an 8,000-word argument for why rising automation doesn't eliminate demand for human work, it increases it. The thesis: AI makes yesterday's expert competence cheap and widely available, which floods every field with output that's close but not quite right—and that creates more demand for the humans who can take it the rest of the way.Dan talked with Brandon about the paradox at the heart of a…
